queries.text:
INSERT INTO user_credentials (username, password_hash)
VALUES ($1, crypt($2, gen_salt('bf')))
queries.name: createUser
comments: Query using pgcrypto for password hashing

queries.text:
SELECT EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM user_credentials
  WHERE username = $1
  AND password_hash = crypt($2, password_hash)
) AS valid
queries.name: verifyPassword
comments: Query using pgcrypto for password verification

queries.text:
SELECT setting_key, setting_value
FROM user_settings
WHERE user_id = $1
queries.name: getUserSettings
comments: Query using settings table (for potential tablefunc pivot)

queries.text:
INSERT INTO user_settings (user_id, setting_key, setting_value)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
ON CONFLICT (user_id, setting_key)
DO UPDATE SET setting_value = EXCLUDED.setting_value
queries.name: setSetting
computeDigest
comments: Test: Simple digest function with 2 parameters returning bytea
Function: digest(data text, algorithm text) → bytea
Expected: Parameters (String, String), Return ByteArray

queries.name: computeHmac
comments: Test: HMAC function with 3 parameters (text, bytea, text) returning bytea
Function: hmac(data text, key bytea, algorithm text) → bytea
Expected: Parameters (String, ByteArray, String), Return ByteArray

queries.name: computeEncodedHash
comments: Test: Nested function calls - encode(digest(...))
Functions: digest(text, text) → bytea, encode(bytea, text) → text
Expected: Parameters (String, String, String), Return String

queries.name: decodeData
comments: Test: decode function - reverse of encode
Function: decode(data text, format text) → bytea
Expected: Parameters (String, String), Return ByteArray

queries.name: generateRandomNumbers
comments: Test: Set-returning function normal_rand with 3 numeric parameters
Function: normal_rand(num_rows int, mean float8, stddev float8) → setof float8
Expected: Parameters (Int, Double, Double), Return Many<Double>

queries.text:
SELECT user_id, setting1, setting2
FROM crosstab($1) AS ct(user_id int, setting1 text, setting2 text)
queries.name: getUserSettingsPivot
comments: Test: crosstab with single parameter and explicit column definitions
Function: crosstab(sql text) → setof record
Expected: Parameters (String), Return Many with structured result

queries.text:
SELECT row_name, category1, category2, category3
FROM crosstab($1, $2) AS ct(row_name text, category1 int, category2 int, category3 int)
queries.name: getUserSettingsByCategory
comments: Test: crosstab with 2 parameters - source and category SQLs
Function: crosstab(source_sql text, category_sql text) → setof record
Expected: Parameters (String, String), Return Many with structured result
